Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal
Off Calendar Jun-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFS JESSICA FRAIDLIN and DEANDRE CURRY'S Motion For Dismissal of class action is granted. No opposition filed and plaintiffs provide a declaration that complies with CRC 3.770(a). Plaintiffs sufficiently explain why they are not pursuing a class action settlement in light of Civil Code sec. 1782(c) and other reasons adduced in their moving papers. Moving party to prepare form of order that tracts the tentative ruling. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/ru

Off Calendar Jan-25-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Seth Safier, Esq. 415-639-9090 x102 for Plaintiff.
James Vorhis, Esq. 415-438-7267 of Nossaman LLP for Defendants.
John Crossman, Esq. and Jessenia Ruiz De La Torre, Esq. 212-223-6700 of Zukerman Gore Brandeis & Crossman for Defendants through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues and cites Kennedy declaration exhibit A. Defendant argues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TENTH AVENUE HOLDINGS, LLC, TENTH AVENUE COMMERCE, LLC's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

DEFENDANT TENTH AVENUE HOLDINGS, LLC's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is Granted for reasons set forth in the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT TENTH AVENUE COMMERCE, LLC's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is Denied. Counsel for Tenth Avenue Commerce has consented to this Court's jurisdiction. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript

Counsel for the Tenth Avenue entities is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Vorhis, Esq. 415-438-7267 of Nossman LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follow:

DEFENDANT TENTH AVENUE HOLDINGS, LLC, TENTH AVENUE COMMERCE, LLC, FROM YOU FLOWERS, LLC'S VERIFIED PETITION TO ADMIT JESENIA RUIZ DELA TORRE AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR DEFENDANTS.

Defendants' application for the pro hac vice admission of Jesenia Ruiz De La Torre is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Order is signed. (Jesenia Ruiz De La Torree, Esq. Zuckerman Gore Brandeis & Crossman LLP, 11 Times Square, 15th Floor, New York, NY 10036, 212-223-6700)(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)
